GeneratedRecipe: bake yellow cake as directed on box in 2 ( 8-inch ) round 8-inch ( 9-inch cakes ) round cake pans. use a large cake pan. let cool, slice one large or larger portion in half to cover bottom of punch bowl. prepare pudding as directed on box. when cake is cooled, add 1/2 can crushed pineapple on top of strawberries. pour half of pudding on cake. next, spread drained pineapple over cake. spread 1 can of strawberries over pudding. spread 1/2 of dream whip over pudding, then spread 1 container cool whip. repeat with remaining pudding. refrigerate 2 hours or until set.

Ingredients: 1/4 c. red wine, 5 whole peppercorns, 1 bay leaf, 1/4 tsp. dried thyme, 1 medium onion, minced, 1 Tbsp. butter, 1 Tbsp. flour, juices collected from roasted duck, salt and pepper, 1/2 c. sour cream
ReferenceRecipe: In saucepan over low heat, cook wine, peppers, bay leaf, thyme and onion until reduced to 2 tablespoons. Blend butter with flour; stir into wine mixture until melted and mixed. Strain through fine sieve. Briskly stir all juices from carved duck into sauce and add sour cream.
GeneratedRecipe: rub inside of duck with salt to taste. melt butter. add onion and cook slowly over medium heat for 15 to 20 minutes. add flour and brown. add wine. bring to a boil and add roasted peppercorns. boil gently for 20 minutes. reduce heat, cover and let simmer for 45 minutes. remove and allow to cool. stir in sour cream and collected. add bay leaf and thyme and thyme and cook, stirring occasionally, until duck are tender. remove duck. serve on collected, garnished with collected and collected.

Ingredients: 2 lb. ground beef, 1 large onion, chopped, 1 large bell pepper, chopped, 2 large cans tomatoes, chopped, 1/2 tsp. garlic powder, 2 large cans chili with hot beans, 3 Tbsp. chili powder, 2 Tbsp. vinegar
ReferenceRecipe: Brown ground beef, stirring	constantly in saucepan. Add onions, tomatoes and bell peppers.	Cook until soft, then add beans, chili powder and garlic powder. After an hour, just serve it up with a freshly tossed salad and crackers.
GeneratedRecipe: brown ground beef; drain. add chopped onion, bell pepper and garlic. mix and add remaining ingredients. simmer for 45 minutes.

Ingredients: 1 1/2 c. finely ground sweet red peppers, 1/4 c. ground hot peppers, 7 1/2 c. sugar, 1 1/2 c. white vinegar, 1 bottle Certo

GeneratedRecipe: in a large saucepan combine peppers and vinegar. bring to a boil and add sugar. add certo and bring to a rolling boil. boil 3 minutes. remove from heat and skim off foam. pour into hot pint jars.

ReferenceRecipe: Melt oleo in a 13 x 9-inch pan. Add crushed Oreos, reserving 3/4 cup for top of dessert. Mix and pat gently for bottom crust. Add softened vanilla ice cream. Smooth over crust; freeze. Cover ice cream layer with fudge sauce. Then sprinkle peanuts. Spread Cool Whip over peanuts. Sprinkle with reserved Oreo cookie crumbs. Freeze. Thaw for 20 minutes before serving.
GeneratedRecipe: crush cookies and mix with melted oleo. press into 9 x 13-inch pan. spread ice cream over crust. spread fudge sauce over ice cream. top with cool whip. sprinkle with peanuts. freeze.

Ingredients: 1 lb. dried black-eyed peas, 2 c. Italian salad dressing, 2 c. diced green pepper, 1 1/2 c. diced onion, 1 c. finely chopped green onion, 1/2 c. finely chopped jalapeno pepper, 3 oz. jar pimento, diced and drained, 1 Tbsp. finely chopped garlic, salt and hot pepper sauce to taste
ReferenceRecipe: Cover peas with water and soak at least 6 hours. Drain well. Put in saucepan and add water to cover. Bring to a boil, reduce heat and allow to simmer until tender (about 45 minutes). Ingredients: 2 eggs, 2/3 c. vegetable shortening, 1 pkg. cake mix (any flavor, any brand), 1/2 c. chopped nuts, 1/2 c. chopped coconut
ReferenceRecipe: Note: Can add other ingredients: raisins, chocolate chips, etc.
GeneratedRecipe: combine cake mix, shortening, eggs, coconut and nuts. mix well. press into a greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan.

Ingredients: 2 deep dish baked pie crusts, 1 can coconut, 8 oz. cream cheese, softened, 1 can Eagle Brand sweetened condensed milk, Smucker's butterscotch topping, 1 c. chopped nuts, 1/4 c. margarine, 16 oz. Cool Whip
ReferenceRecipe: Brown nuts and coconut in margarine to make praline mixture; set aside to cool. Mix softened cream cheese with sweetened condensed milk; fold in Cool Whip. Put 1/4 cream cheese mixture in each pie shell, then add 1/4 praline mixture. Drizzle on 1/4 jar of topping. Repeat layers, ending with cream cheese mixture topped with drizzle of butterscotch topping.
GeneratedRecipe: brown coconut and nuts in margarine. mix cream cheese and sweetened condensed milk. fold in cool whip. pour into pie crusts. freeze.

Ingredients: 3/4 lb. yellow squash, sliced, 1 egg, beaten, 1/3 c. all-purpose flour, 1/3 c. yellow corn meal, 1 tsp. baking powder, 1/2 tsp. salt, 1 medium onion, minced, vegetable oil

Ingredients: 1 lb. low-fat hamburger, 2 large onions, chopped, 1 pkg. taco seasoning, 2 cans stewed tomatoes, 1 can whole kernel corn, 1 can kidney beans
ReferenceRecipe: Crumble, brown and drain hamburger. Add onions; cook until opaque. Add taco seasoning. Mix well before adding tomatoes, corn and beans. If there is not enough liquid for your soup, add tomato juice or tomato sauce. Simmer 10 minutes and serve. Better leftover, flavors blended.

Ingredients: 1 lb. ground ham, 1 1/2 lb. lean ground pork, 1 c. cracker crumbs, pepper, 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce, 2 eggs, beaten, 1 c. milk

Ingredients: 1/4 c. butter, not melted, 1/c. peanut butter, 1 lb. powdered sugar, 1 1/2 tsp. vanilla, 4 Tbsp. milk

Ingredients: 4 c. apple juice, 3 c. grape juice, 1 c. apple cider vinegar

GeneratedRecipe: combine all ingredients and simmer in saucepan for 30 minutes. cool before serving. serve over hot fruit ( about 5 ounces ).

Ingredients: 2 lb. pork sausage, 1 c. bell pepper, finely chopped, 3/4 c. onion, chopped, 2 1/2 c. celery, coarsely chopped, 2 small pkg. instant chicken noodle soup, 4 1/2 c. boiling water, 1 c. rice, uncooked, 1/2 tsp. salt, 1/4 c. butter, melted, 1 c. blanched almonds

Ingredients: 2 pkg. (8 oz.) cream cheese, 6 green onion tops, 1 oz. packaged corned beef slices, diced (4 to 6 slices), 2 Tbsp. Accent, 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire

Ingredients: 2 cans white Shoe Peg corn, well drained, 1 small jar pimentos, chopped, 1/2 c. green pepper, chopped, 1/2 c. red onion, chopped, 2 stalks celery, chopped, 1/3 c. sugar, 1/3 c. vinegar, 1/3 c. oil, 1/2 tsp. salt

Ingredients: 8 oz. cream cheese, 2 (1 lb.) boxes powdered sugar, 1/2 tsp. flavoring as desired, food coloring (to get desired shade)

Ingredients: broccoli (as little or as much as you want), 1 or 2 cans cream of mushroom soup (depending on the amount of broccoli), at least 3 to 4 c. cheese, grated, 1 to 2 lb. sliced fresh mushrooms, crackers *

Ingredients: 1 box yellow cake mix, 4 eggs, 3/4 c. oil, 3/4 c. water, 1 pkg. orange or lemon jello

GeneratedRecipe: mix cake mix, oil, 1 egg and 3/4 cup of water. add lemon jello mix. pour into 3 greased and floured 9 x 13-inch greased and floured pans. bake at 350 degrees for 20 minutes.

Ingredients: 2 chicken pieces, 1/2 sliced onion, seasoning to taste, additional vegetables to taste
ReferenceRecipe: Rinse and dry chicken. Put	onion slices on foil. Season chicken and put on top of onions. Chicken should be skin side up. GeneratedRecipe: fry and put a little butter in skillet; add chicken and onion and fry. sprinkle the chicken pieces over this. add vegetables and stir together until tender. add salt, pepper and pepper to taste.

Ingredients: 1 pkg. strawberry Kool-Aid, 1 pkg. orange Kool-Aid, 1 1/2 c. sugar, 3 1/4 qt. water with ice, 1 (16 oz.) can peach soda, 1 (6 oz.) can frozen orange juice, 1 (12 oz.) can pineapple juice, 1 (12 oz.) bottle ginger ale

Ingredients: 2 c. flour, 2 tsp. baking powder, 3/4 c. margarine, 1 1/4 c. sugar, 2 eggs, beaten, 3/4 c. milk, 1 tsp. vanilla, 1 can cherry pie filling
ReferenceRecipe: Sift together the flour, salt, sugar and baking powder. Mix in margarine as for pie crust. Take out one cup of this mixture for topping. To remainder, add the eggs, milk and vanilla. GeneratedRecipe: sift flour with baking powder, sugar and baking powder. beat together butter and sugar until light and fluffy. add eggs and beat until light and fluffy. blend in milk and vanilla. stir in pie filling. pour into a greased and floured 9 x 13-inch pan.

Ingredients: 1 c. sugar, 1 c. water, 2 Tbsp. red cinnamon drops, 6 medium size firm tart apples, whole cloves
ReferenceRecipe: Combine sugar, water and red cinnamon drops in 1 1/2-quart casserole. Cook about 3 minutes in Radarange oven. Core and pare apples. Stick 3 or 4 cloves into each apple. Ingredients: 1 medium potato, 3 Tbsp. cornmeal, 1 tsp. salt, 1 c. milk, 1/4 tsp. baking soda, 1 1/2 c. boiling water, 2 tsp. sugar, 1 pkg. active dry yeast, 5 1/2 to 6 c. plain flour, 1 large egg white (optional)
ReferenceRecipe: On the day before you undertake this operation, you must start the starter. So peel and finely chop the potato. Place in medium bowl and stir in the boiling water, cornmeal, sugar and salt. Cover and set aside overnight at room temperature.

Ingredients: 1 c. peanut butter, 1 1/4 c. powdered milk, 1 c. light corn syrup, 1 1/4 c. powdered sugar

Ingredients: 3/4 c. butter, 2 (3 oz.) pkg. cream cheese, 4 eggs, 1 1/2 c. sugar, 1 3/4 c. all-purpose flour, 1/2 tsp. baking powder, 1/4 tsp. salt, 1 tsp. vanilla, powdered sugar
ReferenceRecipe: Bring butter, cream cheese and eggs to room temperature, about 1 hour. Grease and flour 9-inch fluted tube pan. Set aside.

Ingredients: 5 lb. smoked boneless ham, 1 lb. dried apricots, 2 c. water, 1/2 c. brown sugar, 1 tsp. allspice
ReferenceRecipe: Soak the ham overnight in enough water to cover it. Refrigerate. (This removes excess salt.)
GeneratedRecipe: cut ham into small bite size pieces and put in a crock-pot with the water. cut apricots diagonally and cover with a thin layer of apricots. add more water. cook at high for 6 hours before serving. add brown sugar and allspice and serve over hot rice.

Ingredients: ham or ham bone, 1 pkg. yellow split peas, 2 large carrots, chopped fine, 1 large onion, 2 sticks celery, chopped fine, green pepper, salt and pepper to taste
ReferenceRecipe: Place ingredients in a large pot. Cover with water (about 6 to 8 cups). Cook on low heat several hours, being sure to add water if necessary.
GeneratedRecipe: cover peas with water and soak overnight. next morning, add ham bone, carrots, onion, celery and green pepper. cook until peas are tender. add salt and pepper to taste.

Ingredients: pecans, butter, salt

GeneratedRecipe: you will also need aluminum foil. spray foil with cooking spray. place pecans on foil. sprinkle with salt and butter. bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown.

Ingredients: 6 c. sugar, 1 tall can milk, 2 sticks butter or oleo, 1 pt. Marshmallow Creme, 1 (12 oz.) jar peanut butter, English or black walnuts (optional)
ReferenceRecipe: Combine sugar, milk and butter in large pan. Put on stove and bring to a boil. Boil for 9 minutes. Stir at all times. Take off stove and add Marshmallow Creme and peanut butter. Stir well; add nuts. Pour in a large buttered cookie sheet. Cut when cool.
GeneratedRecipe: cook sugar, milk and butter to soft ball stage. remove from heat and add peanut butter, marshmallow creme and walnuts. beat until creamy. pour into buttered pan. cool and cut into squares.

Ingredients: 1 raccoon, salt, water

Though often thrown away because of the prejudice that many people have against wild meat.	First skin the coon carefully, then remove the layer of fat, which is often an inch thick and right under the skin.	This fat would	give the meat a disagreeable, oily taste, if left on. But it is nice and white and can be tried out the same as leaf lard, and used for soap.

Ingredients: 8 oz. uncooked egg noodles, 1/2 c. whipping cream or milk, 1/2 tsp. salt, 2 tsp. snipped parsley, 1/2 c. butter/margarine, 3/4 c. grated fresh Parmesan cheese, dash of pepper
ReferenceRecipe: Cook noodles as directed. Drain. Heat margarine and cream over low heat. Stir in cheese, salt and pepper. Pour sauce over hot noodles. Toss to coat. Sprinkle with parsley. Great with scallops or shrimp added.

Ingredients: 2 cans Hawaiian Punch, 2 large bottles ginger ale, ice, 1 qt. raspberry, lime or strawberry sherbet, champagne (optional)

GeneratedRecipe: pour ginger ale over ice in punch bowl. add lime sherbet.

Ingredients: 3 c. crushed strawberries, 6 c. sugar, 2 Tbsp. lemon juice, 2 pkg. Certo

GeneratedRecipe: combine strawberries, sugar and lemon juice in a saucepan. bring to a boil, stirring constantly. boil for 1 minute. remove from heat. stir in certo. skim off foam with metal spoon. pour into sterilized jars and seal.

Ingredients: 1 carton creamed cottage cheese, 1 small carton whipped topping, 1 (3 oz.) pkg. Jell-O, fruit, drained
ReferenceRecipe: Mix cottage cheese with dry Jell-O. Add whipped topping and your favorite fruit. Good combinations are orange Jell-O with mandarin oranges, peaches or pineapple and strawberry Jell-O with fruit cocktail.
GeneratedRecipe: mix cottage cheese and jell-o together. fold in whipped topping and fruit. chill. serves 6 to 8.

Ingredients: 2 boxes Dream Whip, 2 c. powdered sugar, 2 eggs, 1/3 c. soft butter, 1 can crushed pineapple, well drained, 1/2 c. nut meats, graham cracker crumbs
ReferenceRecipe: Mix the Dream Whip as usual; beat until fluffy. Cream together the powdered sugar, eggs and soft butter. Fold the pineapple and nut meats in gently with first mixture. Grease the dish; sprinkle with graham cracker crumbs, top and bottom. Refrigerate; ready to eat.

Ingredients: 1 stick butter, 2 lobster tails (6 oz. and up), defrosted, oil for greasing grill
ReferenceRecipe: Cut bottom of lobster to the shell, but not all the way through. Cut all membrane from under lobster and discard. Break shell so lobster can butterfly, but not completely in half. Brush lobster with butter and place on grill, meat side down, for 2 to 3 minutes. Turn lobster over; brush again with butter and season with garlic salt or preferred seasonings. Cook 7 to 10 minutes, shell side down, or until done, depending on size and heat of grill. Lobster is cooked when meat turns opaque.
GeneratedRecipe: melt butter and oil in skillet. add the lobster tails and saute for 5 minutes. turn down fire while you turn the heat to high and simmer an additional 4 to 5 minutes.

Ingredients: 3 eggs, 1 tsp. granulated sugar, 1/2 tsp. salt, 3/4 c. milk, 8 to 10 slices stale bread, icing sugar for sprinkling
ReferenceRecipe: In a mixing bowl, put eggs, sugar, salt and milk. Mix together thoroughly. Dip bread slices in batter. Fry on lightly greased medium hot pan. Brown on both sides. Serve sprinkled with icing sugar and fresh strawberries for a special treat.
GeneratedRecipe: beat the eggs; then add milk and sugar and blend well. add salt and icing. dip bread slices in batter and fry. drain on paper towel.

Ingredients: 2 bunches carrots, cut diagonally into 1-inch pieces and cooked, 1/2 c. salad oil, 1/2 c. cider vinegar, 1/2 c. plus 2 Tbsp. sugar, 1 can tomato soup (undiluted), 1 large onion, chopped, 1 green pepper, chopped
ReferenceRecipe: Mix sauce; pour it over carrots while hot. Serve cold.
GeneratedRecipe: combine soup, oil, vinegar, sugar and pepper in a saucepan. bring to a boil. pour over carrots, green pepper and onion. cover and refrigerate overnight.

Ingredients: 1 c. boiling water, 1/4 c. Crisco, 1/2 c. sugar, 1 tsp. salt, 1 c. evaporated milk, 1 pkg. yeast in 1/2 c. lukewarm water, 2 eggs, beaten, 7 1/2 c. all-purpose flour (approximately)
ReferenceRecipe: Pour hot water over Crisco and sugar. Stir and cool to lukewarm. Dissolve yeast in 1/2 cup lukewarm water and stir in cooled mixture. Add beaten eggs and milk. GeneratedRecipe: dissolve yeast in lukewarm water. add sugar, salt and crisco. cool to lukewarm. add milk, eggs and flour. mix well. cover and let rise until double in bulk.

Ingredients: 2 lb. Eckrich smoked sausage, cut in small pieces, 1/2 c. vinegar, 1/2 c. warm water, 4 heaping Tbsp. brown sugar, 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce, 1 tsp. mustard
ReferenceRecipe: Place sausage pieces in crock-pot. Mix all other ingredients and pour over sausage. Start crock-pot on high, turn back to low for 4 to 6 hours.
GeneratedRecipe: mix brown sugar, warm water, vinegar, mustard, worcestershire sauce and warm water. add sausage; bring to a boil. simmer for 10 minutes. add sausage and heat. serve on toothpicks.

Ingredients: 6 medium potatoes, cooked and diced, 6 hard-boiled eggs, peeled and diced, 2 medium onions, grated, 1/2 c. diced sweet pickles, 1 c. Hellmann's mayonnaise, 1/4 c. olives, 1/4 c. sweet peppers, 2 tsp. sugar, salt and pepper to taste, 1 Tbsp. prepared mustard, 1 Tbsp. vinegar
ReferenceRecipe: Combine first five ingredients. Mix remaining ingredients. Stir well, until smooth. Pour over first set of ingredients and stir lightly until mixed well. Garnish with hard-cooked eggs.
GeneratedRecipe: combine potatoes, eggs, onions, pickles, olives and olives. mix mayonnaise, sugar, vinegar, mustard and salt and pepper. pour over potato mixture and mix well. chill.

Ingredients: 1 1/2 to 2 lb. stew beef, 1 pkg. onion soup mix, 1/2 stalk celery, 4 carrots, cut up, 5 to 6 medium size potatoes, cut and cubed, 1/2 c. flour, salt and pepper to taste
ReferenceRecipe: In a crock-pot, put stew beef, potatoes, carrots and celery. Stir. Also add remaining ingredients. Stir occasionally until all beef is cooked through and potatoes and carrots are easy to cut. Start crock-pot early in morning to give stew beef plenty of time to cook.
GeneratedRecipe: brown stew beef in a large dutch oven. add onion soup mix, carrots and celery. cover and simmer 1 1/2 hours or until meat is tender. add potatoes and carrots. cook until vegetables are tender. thicken with flour, salt and pepper.

Ingredients: 3 Tbsp. margarine, 3 Tbsp. flour, 1/4 tsp. salt, 1 dash cayenne pepper, 1 1/2 c. milk, 1/4 c. Parmesan cheese
ReferenceRecipe: Melt margarine. Stir in flour, salt and cayenne. Cook for 1 minute. Remove. Stir in milk until mixture is smooth. Cook over low heat, stirring until thick. Add cheese.
GeneratedRecipe: melt margarine in saucepan. blend in flour, salt and cayenne. cook over low heat for 2 minutes, stirring constantly. gradually stir in milk. cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. cook and stir 1 minute longer. remove from heat. stir in parmesan cheese and cayenne pepper. serve over noodles or pasta.

Ingredients: 10 medium potatoes, cut in small cubes, 1 can evaporated milk, 3 Tbsp. butter, salt, 8 slices cooked bacon, 1/2 c. onion, diced
ReferenceRecipe: In large soup pan saute bacon, onion and celery until tender. Add cubed potatoes; cover with water and boil until potatoes are tender. Drain water until it covers potatoes. Add can of evaporated milk and butter. Mash potatoes and warm soup. Serve hot.
GeneratedRecipe: in small skillet, cook bacon until crisp and crumble. drain. saute onion in butter until tender. add potatoes and evaporated milk, salt and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until mixture is heated through.

Ingredients: 3 c. flour, 5 Tbsp. ice water, 1 egg, well beaten, 1 tsp. salt, 1 1/4 c. shortening, 1 Tbsp. vinegar
